creator: imb@protected-networks.net
text:
(In reply to Christos Margiolis from comment #25)

Sadly .. no. I also tried adding the CODEC ID from the linux log entry, which would seem to be required but still nothing. That was ..

diff --git a/sys/dev/sound/pci/hda/hdac.h b/sys/dev/sound/pci/hda/hdac.h
index 2a79cb47353..b6b2cc44342 100644
--- a/sys/dev/sound/pci/hda/hdac.h
+++ b/sys/dev/sound/pci/hda/hdac.h
@@ -921,6 +921,7 @@
 #define HDA_CODEC_INTELALLK    HDA_CODEC_CONSTRUCT(INTEL, 0x2815)
 #define HDA_CODEC_INTELJLK     HDA_CODEC_CONSTRUCT(INTEL, 0x281a)
 #define HDA_CODEC_INTELELLK    HDA_CODEC_CONSTRUCT(INTEL, 0x281b)
+#define HDA_CODEC_INTELRPLK    HDA_CODEC_CONSTRUCT(INTEL, 0x281f)
 #define HDA_CODEC_INTELCT      HDA_CODEC_CONSTRUCT(INTEL, 0x2880)
 #define HDA_CODEC_INTELVV2     HDA_CODEC_CONSTRUCT(INTEL, 0x2882)
 #define HDA_CODEC_INTELBR      HDA_CODEC_CONSTRUCT(INTEL, 0x2883)
diff --git a/sys/dev/sound/pci/hda/hdacc.c b/sys/dev/sound/pci/hda/hdacc.c
index 4198982c9c2..e95b3a58546 100644
--- a/sys/dev/sound/pci/hda/hdacc.c
+++ b/sys/dev/sound/pci/hda/hdacc.c
@@ -386,6 +386,7 @@ static const struct {
        { HDA_CODEC_INTELKBLK, 0,       "Intel Kaby Lake" },
        { HDA_CODEC_INTELJLK, 0,        "Intel Jasper Lake" },
        { HDA_CODEC_INTELELLK, 0,       "Intel Elkhart Lake" },
+       { HDA_CODEC_INTELRPLK, 0,       "Intel Raptor Lake" },
        { HDA_CODEC_INTELCT, 0,         "Intel Cedar Trail" },
        { HDA_CODEC_INTELVV2, 0,        "Intel Valleyview2" },
        { HDA_CODEC_INTELBR, 0,         "Intel Braswell" },
